cards.definition: to put thoughts, feelings, or ideas into words or actions
to give an account of something in words
to tell someone something; to give official or necessary knowledge
to reply or react to something said or done
to make something known formally or publicly
to refer to something briefly or in passing
to persuade someone to believe something or take a course of action
to give special importance or prominence to something
to make something easier to understand by explaining it more clearly
a polite or formal way to ask for something
to put forward an idea for consideration without insisting on it
to establish that something is true, or to make an arrangement definite
to state that something is not true; to refuse to acknowledge or give
to acknowledge something true, especially something difficult or unwelcome
to suggest something as good or suitable, usually with reasons
to share or exchange information, ideas, or feelings with others
a brief remark or observation made in response to something; to make such a remark
the quality or feeling conveyed by someone's voice, writing, or manner
a group of words that work together as a unit; to express something in particular words
to repeat exactly what someone has said or written, with credit; an instance of this
to look over and improve something, especially a piece of writing
to speak to someone directly; or to deal with a problem or topic head-on
worried or uneasy about what might happen
trusting in your own abilities or the truth of something
feeling blocked or thwarted in your goals or efforts
feeling ease or comfort after tension or anxiety has passed
eager to know or learn about something
firmly committed to achieving a goal despite obstacles
completely drained of energy or strength
feeling that demands or emotions are more than you can handle
expecting good outcomes, even in difficult circumstances
expecting bad or negative outcomes
quietly satisfied and at peace with your situation
in a state of pain, worry, or great suffering
feeling bitter anger because of perceived unfair treatment
feeling or showing thanks for what someone has done or given
driven by a strong desire to act or achieve something
pausing before acting because of uncertainty or doubt
unwilling to do something, even if you eventually do it
the general emotional state of a person at a given time
a settled way of thinking or feeling about something
a strong feeling arising from your circumstances or relationships
the ability to wait or endure difficulty without complaint
the ability to accept or endure what is difficult or different
happening often, at short intervals
happening now and then, but not regularly
coming before in time or order; the formal register of 'before'
coming immediately before the current one
belonging to or happening in the present time
having happened not long ago
happening right now or without any delay
lasting only for a limited time, not permanent
lasting indefinitely; not temporary or changeable
a planned sequence of events or tasks with times attached
the latest time or date by which something must be done
a regular sequence of actions; or ordinary and repeated
a distinct period or stage in a process or development
the length of time that something lasts
a series of things or events in a particular order
not yet resolved, completed, or decided
continuing to happen or develop at the present time
happening or arriving at some later time, often after difficulty
correct in all details; free from error
showing the characteristics expected of a category or group
important enough to be worth noticing or considering
achieving maximum results with minimum waste of time or effort
producing the intended result; working as planned
suitable or right for a particular situation
having the ability or qualities needed to do something
consistently doing what is expected; able to be trusted
able to adapt easily to different circumstances
knowing about or conscious of something
clearly defined and precise, not vague or general
concerned with real-world application rather than theory
absolutely necessary; of the most basic and important nature
the capacity to develop, improve, or achieve something
able to be used, obtained, or reached
easily seen or understood; not needing explanation
consisting of many interconnected parts; not simple
recognizably different from other things; clearly separate
having a resemblance to something else without being identical
truly what it is claimed to be; authentic
always behaving in the same way or maintaining the same standard
satisfactory for a particular purpose, but not exceptional
not extreme in degree; occupying a middle position
of extreme strength or degree; highly concentrated
a person of the same age, status, or ability as another
a person one works with; the professional word for coworker
an experienced person who guides and advises someone less experienced
the power to give orders and make decisions; also a recognized expert
a single human person, especially when considered separately from a group
a group of people sharing a place, background, or common interests
the people for whom a piece of communication is intended
a person who competes against or argues against another
a person who lives in a particular place on a long-term basis
a legally recognized member of a state or country
a person who freely chooses to do something, especially without pay
a person who oversees and directs the work of others
a person seeking a position, role, or qualification
a person who speaks or acts on behalf of others
a person with deep skill or knowledge in a particular area
a person who takes part in something
a serious disagreement, struggle, or clash
the act of working together toward a shared goal
a situation in which people or groups rival each other for the same goal
the way in which two or more people or things are connected
to think carefully about something before deciding or acting
to become fully aware of something previously not understood
to accept something as true without checking or proving it
to reach a judgment or decision after thinking or investigating
to identify something from previous knowledge; or to acknowledge something
to concentrate attention or effort on one specific thing
to examine something in detail to understand its structure or meaning
to think deeply and carefully about something, especially past experience
to say what will happen before it happens, based on available information
to assess the quality, significance, or value of something
to examine the similarities and differences between two or more things
to examine something again; or a formal assessment of something
a decision or assessment reached after careful consideration; or the capacity to make good decisions
a personal view or assessment not necessarily based on fact
a particular way of thinking about or viewing something
the act of directing your mind to something; concentration
the reason for which something is done or for which something exists
what someone means to do or achieve; a planned aim
a belief or assumption that something will happen
an effect or image produced in the mind by something experienced
to understand something fully and completely
to explain or understand the meaning of something
a difficult situation requiring skill; or to dispute or question something
something that stands in the way of progress
to settle a problem or disagreement; or to decide firmly on a course of action
to try to do something, especially something that may not succeed
to stop something from happening before it occurs
to handle a situation, task, or group of people effectively
to succeed in dealing with or defeating a difficulty
to succeed in reaching a goal through effort
to make great effort against difficulty; or the experience of doing so
the use of energy and determination to do something; a serious attempt
forward movement toward a goal or better state
an answer or way of dealing with a problem
a plan carefully designed to achieve a long-term goal
a way of dealing with something; or to come near to something
a result or effect that follows from an action or event
the possibility of something bad or harmful happening
a favorable set of circumstances that makes something possible
the final result or consequence of a process or situation
the smallest possible or permitted amount
the largest possible or permitted amount
more than half of a total; the greater part
less than half of a total; the smaller part
a part or share of a larger whole
the relationship of one part to another or to the whole
enough for a particular purpose; as much as is needed
to roughly calculate or judge the value, number, or amount of something
close to the correct value or amount, but not exact
a typical or middle amount, often calculated by adding and dividing
the spread between the lowest and highest values; the full extent of something
one of several elements that contribute to a result
an amount or number of something
more than is needed, normal, or permitted
a situation in which there is not enough of something
the complete amount when everything is added together
a small part taken as representative of the whole
a measure of one quantity against another, usually over time
the relative size or extent of something; the degree of something
facts and statistics collected and used for analysis or reference
to arrange things systematically so they work well together
to make yourself or something ready for what is coming
to set something up securely; to put something in place on a firm basis
to keep something in a consistent state; to preserve or continue something
to make something better than it was
to grow or cause to grow into something more advanced
to put knowledge or effort into practical use; or to submit a formal request
to carefully choose from a range of options
to establish exactly what something is or what its nature is
to carry out an action, task, or function
to function or cause something to function, especially a machine or system
to need something as a necessary condition; to demand something officially
to supply or make available what is needed
to make, create, or bring something into existence
to have an impact or influence on something
to include something as a necessary or integral part
to make something part of a larger group or whole
to investigate a subject systematically to discover facts or reach conclusions
to give a brief account of the main points of something
a general plan or description showing the main points; to present something in this way
to correct and improve a piece of writing or other work
the way in which something is arranged or presented
a distinct section of a piece of writing that develops one idea
information or facts that support or prove a claim
the origin or place from which information comes
a specific, small piece of information or a feature of something
a specific instance or case used to illustrate a general point
a class or group of things sharing common characteristics
the subject of a piece of writing, conversation, or study
a matter of concern, dispute, or importance
a reason or set of reasons given in support of a claim or position
a judgment or decision reached at the end of a reasoning process
a preliminary version of a piece of writing, not yet final
to state the precise meaning of a word, concept, or thing
a system of government in which power is held by the people, directly or through elected representatives
laws passed by a legislative body; the process of making laws
a formal change or addition made to a law, constitution, or document
an official set of principles or rules guiding decisions and actions
an official rule or directive issued by an authority to control behavior
the official authority over a geographic area or type of legal case
relating to a system of government in which states unite under a central authority
relating to the local government of a city or town
the form or system by which votes are cast in an election
a formal written request signed by many people, addressed to an authority
the body of people who make and change laws in a country or state
the branch of government that implements and enforces laws; or relating to leadership functions
relating to courts, judges, and the system of justice
a formal decision made in a legal case by a judge or jury
a person accused of a crime or sued in a court case
a formal spoken or written statement, especially in a legal proceeding
an official count or survey of a population and its characteristics
a formal agreement made between two or more countries or parties
relating to or in accordance with a constitution; permitted by the constitution
relating to citizens and their concerns; or conducted according to accepted social norms
legal or moral entitlements that all people are owed
an official authorization or requirement; or the authority given by voters to a leader
the state of having paid work; the process of hiring someone
a legally binding agreement between two or more parties
to reach an agreement through discussion and compromise
money paid in exchange for work, loss, or injury
an advantage provided to employees beyond salary; or any positive outcome
something one is required or bound to do, by law, duty, or promise
legal responsibility for something, especially for damages or debts
to act in accordance with rules, laws, or requests
a qualification, certificate, or achievement that proves someone's competence
official recognition that someone has met a required standard
a person who can speak to your character or ability, especially for a job
the act of formally leaving a job or position by choice
to formally end an employment or contractual relationship
a division of a large organization responsible for a specific function
the people employed by an organization; the formal word for staff
a person with expert qualifications in a skilled field; or meeting the standards of a profession
relating to a large company or the culture of large organizations
to raise someone to a higher job or rank; or to publicize something
to watch over and direct the work of others
to formally give up a job or position by choice
a plan for spending and managing money over a period of time
money spent on something; a cost incurred
money received regularly from work, investment, or other sources
the total income earned by a business or government before costs
the money remaining after all costs and expenses have been paid
the amount by which spending exceeds income; a shortfall
to put money, time, or effort into something with the expectation of a return
money owed to another person, bank, or institution
the ability to borrow money based on trust; or a positive balance in an account
to subtract an amount from a total, especially for tax or accounting purposes
a compulsory payment to the government, based on income, purchases, or property
the general rise in prices over time, reducing the purchasing power of money
a period of economic decline, usually marked by falling output and rising unemployment
an instance of buying, selling, or exchanging money or goods
a sum of money borrowed, usually with interest, to be repaid over time
a loan secured against property, repaid over many years
a contract providing financial protection against loss in exchange for regular payments
the regular amount paid for insurance; or something of higher quality or price
the additional cost paid on borrowed money, or the return earned on savings
a record of financial transactions; a deposit account at a bank
the identification of an illness or problem based on its signs and symptoms
a sign or effect that indicates the presence of an illness or problem
persisting for a long time, or recurring frequently; the opposite of acute
a doctor's written instruction for a specific medication or treatment
the prescribed amount of a medicine to be taken at one time or over a period
protected against infection or a disease; or not affected by something
the process of obtaining and using the food necessary for health
treatment for a physical, mental, or behavioral condition
a medical doctor, especially one who treats general illnesses
requiring immediate attention or action
a sudden, serious situation requiring immediate action
action taken to stop something bad from happening before it occurs
voluntary agreement to something, especially given with full understanding
a persistent feeling of worry and unease, often without a specific cause
a medical condition causing persistent low mood, loss of energy, and reduced functioning
the chemical processes in the body that convert food into energy
having an immune system reaction to a substance that is harmless to most people
capable of spreading from one person to another through contact or the air
the channels of mass communication, including television, radio, newspapers, and online platforms
a person who researches, writes, and reports news and information professionally
a newspaper article expressing the publication's opinion; or relating to editorial judgment
the title of a news article, written to attract attention and summarize the story
a tendency to favor one side, perspective, or outcome over others
the quality of being trusted and believed
to transmit information to a wide audience via television, radio, or online channels
the extent and manner in which a news event is reported
a book, magazine, or other printed or digital work that has been issued for public distribution
information designed to promote a political viewpoint, often misleading or one-sided
the suppression of information, speech, or media by an authority
not identified by name; of unknown identity
a prolonged public disagreement or debate about something
a claim that someone has done something wrong, made without proof
a formal or official declaration made publicly or in a legal context
openness and clarity about how decisions are made and how things work
to confirm the truth or accuracy of something through investigation
a story or way of presenting events that shapes how they are understood
a digital or physical space used to publish, share, or promote content and ideas
the number of copies of a publication distributed; or the spread of information
to give official permission for something to happen
to present something formally for consideration or approval
to formally inform someone of something
to confirm that something has been received or recognized
to reveal or make known something that was private or hidden
to voluntarily give up a right, claim, or requirement
legally or officially acceptable; still in effect
having no legal force or effect; to cancel an agreement
to reach the end of the period for which something is valid
a punishment or financial charge imposed for breaking a rule or law
an act that breaks a law, rule, or agreement
a formal request to change or review a decision; or to make such a request
official documents that prove identity, support a claim, or record an agreement
a formal written request for a position, benefit, or official recognition
an official document granting permission to do something; to allow something
to record something officially; or an official record or list
having the legal or moral right to something
legally responsible for something
required by law or authority; not optional
not required to follow a rule or pay a charge that others must
the subjects, content, and materials that make up a course of study
the detailed plan and schedule for a course, including topics and assessment
the evaluation of someone's work, knowledge, or abilities
a proposed explanation for something that can be tested through investigation
a specific goal or target to be achieved; or not influenced by personal feelings
in academic use, the body of published research and writing on a subject
to refer to a source, author, or piece of evidence as support for a claim
to restate something in your own words while preserving the original meaning
cards.syn: restate, rephrase, express in your own words

cards.definition: using someone else's work or ideas as your own without acknowledgment
to add explanatory notes or comments to a text
the ability to understand and interpret what has been read or heard
able to speak, read, or write a language with ease and accuracy
the central argument or claim of an essay or research paper
a short summary at the start of a research paper outlining its content and findings
a list of sources cited or consulted in writing a paper or book
a detailed examination of something to understand its structure, meaning, or implications
the standards or rules by which something is judged or decided (plural of criterion)
a small group meeting for discussion, instruction, or advanced study
serious academic study and achievement; or a financial award to support education
the process of reviewing and improving a draft of writing
fairness that accounts for different needs, as opposed to treating everyone identically
the unequal distribution of opportunity, resources, or rights among people
unjust treatment of people based on their membership in a group
an unearned advantage held by some people due to their social position or identity
a fixed, oversimplified belief about a group of people
a preconceived and usually unfavorable opinion not based on reason or experience
the practice of ensuring all people can participate and belong, regardless of background
the presence of variety among people in terms of background, identity, and experience
the state of lacking sufficient material resources for a basic standard of living
the health, happiness, and financial security of a person or group; or government support for those in need
all the people living in a place; or a specific group studied in research
all the people born and living at approximately the same time
the shared values, customs, beliefs, and arts of a group of people
traditions, culture, or property passed down from earlier generations
the process of coming to live permanently in a foreign country
a person who has been forced to leave their country to escape danger or persecution
who someone is — their sense of self, defined by characteristics, values, and belonging
a person's relative social, professional, or legal position
the process of adopting the culture and values of a new community or country
a long-established custom or practice passed down through generations
relating to technology that uses data in the form of binary code
a system of connected people, devices, or organizations
a set of rules or instructions followed by a computer to solve a problem or make a decision
the right to keep personal information and personal life free from public scrutiny
a substance, especially a gas, released into the environment
able to be naturally replenished and not permanently depleted by use
able to continue over time without depleting resources or causing lasting harm
the careful protection and management of natural resources and environments
a community of living organisms and their physical environment, functioning together
the typical pattern of weather in a region over a long period; or the broader atmospheric conditions of the planet
the fundamental systems that a society or organization depends on to function
relating to a city or densely populated area
relating to the countryside or areas outside cities and towns
a supply of something useful — material, financial, or human — that can be drawn on
the introduction of harmful substances or contaminants into the environment
the introduction of a new idea, method, or product that improves on what existed before
the ability or right to reach, use, or enter something
relating to or involving the whole world
protection from threat, danger, or loss — physical, digital, or financial
the application of scientific knowledge to create tools, systems, or processes
to publicly support a cause or policy; or a person who does so
to formally or actively be against something
to put forward an idea, plan, or suggestion for consideration
a formal discussion of opposing viewpoints on a topic; to engage in such discussion
a disagreement or argument, especially a formal or prolonged one; to contest a claim
to show or argue that something is reasonable or right
to point out the faults or shortcomings of something or someone
cards.syn: challenge, find fault with, evaluate negatively

cards.definition: to argue in support of someone or something; or to protect from attack
to respond to an argument with an opposing one; a response or challenge
to acknowledge that a point or argument made by someone else has merit
a statement or assumption used as the basis of an argument
a confident statement of something as fact, without necessarily offering proof
a reason given for disagreeing with or opposing something
a response that disproves or counters an argument with evidence or reasoning
a person's position or attitude on a particular issue
a change made to improve a system or institution
to express strong public objection to something; an act of such objection
the obligation to answer for one's actions and decisions to others
general agreement reached among a group, often through discussion and compromise
an agreement in which both sides give up something to reach a middle ground
an error in reasoning that makes an argument invalid, even if it sounds convincing
cards.syn: error in reasoning, logical flaw, misconception

cards.definition: something suggested or hinted at by a statement or action without being directly stated
a conclusion reached through reasoning from evidence, not stated outright
a direct relationship in which one thing brings about another
a relationship in which two things change together, without one necessarily causing the other
to say or show that something is inconsistent or opposite to another claim
to prove that a claim is wrong by presenting contrary evidence
to provide evidence that supports and confirms a claim
to confirm a piece of evidence by providing independent additional evidence
to reach a conclusion by applying known principles to a specific situation
moving from specific observations toward a broader general conclusion
to form a theory or opinion about something without full evidence
based on observation, experiment, or measured evidence rather than theory or assumption
based on individual personal accounts rather than systematic evidence or data
based on reason and logic rather than emotion, habit, or impulse
logically consistent and clearly expressed, with each part fitting together
seeming reasonable and probably true, though not yet proven
a conclusion or opinion formed on the basis of incomplete evidence
stated clearly and directly, with nothing left implied or assumed
to suggest or assume something as a starting point for reasoning, before proof
a statement or idea put forward for consideration, argument, or debate
proving something so decisively that no further doubt or argument is possible
to take something for granted with more confidence than the evidence warrants
a statement that restates itself rather than adding new information, appearing to prove something while saying nothing
a conclusion that does not logically follow from the argument or evidence that preceded it
a subtle difference in meaning, expression, or feeling that changes how something is understood
so delicate or gradual as to be difficult to detect or describe
suggested or understood without being directly stated
able to be understood in more than one way, creating uncertainty
to recognize or show what makes two things different from one another
to identify or explain the specific differences between two or more things
similar in function or structure to something else, even if different in form
equal in value, meaning, or effect, though not necessarily identical
considered in comparison to something else rather than on its own terms
based on personal feelings, interpretation, or perspective rather than external facts
exactly as stated, without metaphor, exaggeration, or interpretation
using metaphor, symbolism, or other non-literal expression to convey meaning
existing in name or title only, not in actual substance or function
existing only at the surface level, lacking depth or real understanding
existing as a permanent and essential quality of something, not acquired from outside
belonging to something by its very nature, not imposed or added from outside
a corresponding similarity in structure or situation between two different things
to share common elements without being identical; the area of shared ground
a division into two contrasting or mutually exclusive categories
applying absolutely, without exceptions or qualification
not qualified, restricted, or relative in any way; unconditional
a continuous range between two extremes, along which things can be placed
a structured set of ideas or rules used as the basis for approaching a problem or making judgments
a system in which things or people are ranked one above another in order of importance or authority
to assign something to a category based on its shared characteristics
to describe the essential or defining qualities of something
the range or extent of what something covers, includes, or addresses
a factor or limit that defines the conditions or boundaries of something
the point at which a condition changes, an effect begins, or a limit is reached
a standard or reference point against which things are measured or compared
one part of a larger system or whole that contributes to its overall function
a distinct aspect of something that can be considered separately as part of a fuller analysis
a factor that can change across conditions and whose changes may affect outcomes
a factor that remains unchanged across conditions; something fixed and reliable
the standard or expected pattern of behavior in a given context or group
a starting point used for comparison when measuring change or evaluating progress
carried out according to a fixed, organized plan or method, step by step
extremely thorough, careful, and precise, with no shortcuts taken
covering all aspects of something completely, leaving nothing important out
coming before the main event or action; preparatory and subject to revision
building up over time through a series of successive additions
a notable difference between things that should match or be consistent
a single standard or rule by which something is judged or decided (singular of criteria)
a system for classifying things into ordered, named categories with an explicit structure behind it
not yet certain or fixed; offered cautiously as a starting point, not a final position
arranged or accepted for the present time but liable to be changed later
depending on a specific condition being met; not unconditional or absolute
based on a supposed situation rather than an actual one; used for exploring possibilities
relating to a set of ideas or principles rather than their practical application
a warning or qualification attached to a statement that limits or conditions its application
a case to which a general rule does not apply, requiring separate treatment
a condition or weakness that restricts what something can show, do, or achieve
a condition, reservation, or modification added to a statement to make it more precise
of doubtful quality or truth; arousing suspicion or uncertainty
causing disagreement among reasonable people; likely to provoke argument or dispute
open to genuine argument and disagreement; not yet settled
holding contradictory feelings or attitudes about something at the same time
existing as an idea or concept rather than as a physical, observable thing
something that deviates from what is standard, expected, or normal
to extend conclusions from known data to situations or ranges beyond what was directly observed
to draw broad conclusions from a limited number of specific cases
cards.syn: overgeneralize, draw broad conclusions, infer widely

cards.definition: not leading to a clear or definite answer; leaving the question open
presenting something complex in oversimplified terms that lose or distort what matters
open to debate and with no clear answer; or no longer practically relevant
the quality of being honest and consistently acting in accordance with strong moral principles
a fundamental truth or rule that guides behavior and underlies more specific rules
the right or capacity to make one's own decisions without external control
a situation requiring a choice between two options that are both difficult or unappealing
the quality of being genuinely good or worthy, judged on its own terms rather than by status or popularity
responsibility for a fault or wrongdoing, assessed in terms of how much someone is to blame
a person's inner sense of what is right and wrong, guiding behavior independently of external rules
cards.syn: moral sense, inner voice, ethical judgment

cards.definition: the reason why someone does something, especially something significant or controversial
based on fair and morally right behavior; giving people what they deserve
involved in wrongdoing, even if not the main actor, through participation, silence, or inaction
failing to take the reasonable care that a situation requires, resulting in harm or risk
to force someone to act through threats, pressure, or circumstances that remove genuine choice
required to answer for one's decisions and actions to those affected by them
open and clear about how decisions are made and what is actually happening
given, felt, or done equally by both sides; mutual in nature
corresponding in scale to the circumstances; matching the size of the response to the size of the situation
not favoring any particular side; treating all parties fairly and without bias
a previous case or decision used as a guide or justification for similar future situations
the practice of claiming moral standards or beliefs that one's own behavior contradicts
convenient and practical in the short term, though possibly at the expense of principle
to change something completely in character or form, not just partially improve it
to change gradually and continuously through a series of small developments over time
to adjust to new conditions in a way that maintains function or viability
the process of changing from one state or condition to another, including the journey itself
to become progressively worse in quality, condition, or function
to increase in speed, rate, or intensity
to make or become less likely to change, collapse, or deteriorate further
to rise and fall irregularly over time, without a clear trend in either direction
to come into being or become visible, especially from a complex or uncertain situation
to combine separate parts or systems into a unified whole that functions together
to interrupt the normal course of a system or process in a significant way
to keep something going over time by maintaining the conditions it needs to continue
to continue firmly in a course of action despite difficulty or pressure to stop
to happen again, especially in a recognizable pattern
a general direction in which something is changing or developing over time
the path or arc something follows as it develops through time
a recurring sequence of events that returns to something close to its starting point
the force or energy of an ongoing process, which resists both starting and stopping
something that causes or significantly speeds up a change without being consumed by it
to stop developing or making progress, remaining in a fixed state without renewal
to return to a previous, often worse, state or behavior
to increase by accumulation; or to make something worse by adding to it over time
to become aware of something through the senses or the mind, often with active attention
to evaluate the nature, quality, or significance of something through careful examination
to examine something very closely and critically, not accepting appearances at face value
a careful analysis that identifies both strengths and weaknesses; not just criticism
to combine separate pieces of information or ideas into a coherent new whole
to place something in its surrounding circumstances so that it can be properly understood
to present a situation in a particular way that shapes how it is understood by others
to rank things in order of importance and allocate attention and resources accordingly
to consider the relative importance or value of competing factors before deciding
to investigate something deeply and searchingly, going beneath the surface
to question something closely and critically, including the assumptions behind it
to draw out and examine the separate components and assumptions within something
cards.ant: summarize, compress, accept at face value

cards.definition: to express something clearly and precisely in words, making explicit what was vague
to create or express something in a systematic and precise form
to place two things side by side in order to reveal their differences or relationship
cards.syn: compare side by side, set against, contrast

cards.definition: to express or measure something as a number or quantity, making it concrete and comparable
cards.syn: measure, calculate, put a number on

cards.definition: to add conditions or limitations to a statement in order to make it more accurate
to recognize or perceive something that requires attention or insight to detect
to require something to be true as a prior condition; to assume something inside an argument
to make apparently contradictory facts, positions, or feelings compatible with each other
a state of opposition between two forces, ideas, or values that resist easy resolution
a balance achieved by accepting less of one thing in order to gain more of another
to complete or enhance something else by supplying what it lacks
to strengthen an idea, behavior, or structure by adding support to it
to weaken something gradually, often from within or without open confrontation
to cancel out or make something ineffective or null
to alter something so that it no longer accurately represents what it describes
to describe something complex in terms so simple that important meaning is lost
to treat as identical two things that are actually different, blurring an important distinction
cards.syn: merge, confuse, treat as the same

cards.definition: contrary to what one would naturally expect or instinctively assume
a statement or situation that seems contradictory but may reveal a deeper truth on examination
a comparison that explains something by showing its structural similarity to something already understood
a situation or expression in which what appears to be the case is very different from the reality
to suggest something without stating it directly; the speaker's side of the imply/infer pair
to draw a conclusion from evidence or implication without it being explicitly stated
to act in accordance with prevailing standards, expectations, or norms of a group
to adjust or make room for something, especially differing needs or constraints
cards.syn: adapt to, make room for, adjust

cards.definition: to make something bad worse by adding to it or failing to address its cause
to make something harmful or severe less so, without necessarily eliminating it
to depart from an established course, standard, or expectation
to divide something sharply into two opposing extremes, making the middle ground harder to hold
the art of using language effectively and persuasively to communicate and influence
the choice and use of words and tone in speech or writing
the emotional or cultural meaning a word carries beyond its literal dictionary definition
the underlying meaning or message beneath what is explicitly stated
a mild or indirect expression used to soften something harsh, uncomfortable, or blunt
specialized vocabulary used by a particular group, which can clarify within the group and exclude outside it
the everyday spoken language of a particular group or place, as opposed to a formal or written register
an indirect reference to something assumed to be known, adding meaning without naming it directly
the arrangement of words and phrases to create well-formed, meaningful sentences
cards.syn: sentence structure, grammar, arrangement of words

cards.definition: to suggest something negative or damaging in an indirect, subtle way
used in ordinary, informal conversation rather than in formal speech or writing
expressing what needs to be said in the fewest words necessary, without loss of meaning
using more words than are needed, to the detriment of clarity and the reader's patience
expressing ideas fluently, persuasively, and with a quality of beauty that moves the listener
the quality of being logically consistent and clearly connected throughout
effective at convincing others through the quality and presentation of reasoning
the quality of being open to more than one interpretation, creating productive or problematic uncertainty
the level of formality or style of language appropriate to a particular situation or audience
to soften or qualify a claim by adding careful conditions, reducing commitment to it
carrying a strong emotional or political charge that goes beyond a word's literal meaning
the way a situation or issue is presented so as to shape how people understand and respond to it
to describe something as less significant or extreme than it actually is, whether from modesty or strategy
to describe something as more significant or extreme than it actually is
showing careful attention to subtle distinctions and complex meaning
the literal, dictionary meaning of a word, independent of any emotional or cultural association
to state something confidently and directly as a fact, without necessarily offering proof
to spread an idea, belief, or piece of information widely across people or channels
a figure of speech that describes something by directly calling it something else, without using like or as
the ability to understand and share what another person is feeling, from inside their experience
sorrow or concern for another person's pain or misfortune, felt from the outside
empathy or sympathy combined with a genuine desire to help or relieve suffering
sorrow felt for someone in a worse situation than yourself, often from a position of superiority
shared commitment to a common cause or struggle, especially between equals
comfort given to someone in distress or grief, especially through words or presence
to express shared sympathy for a difficulty that both parties have experienced or understand
emotional distance from a situation, allowing calm judgment without personal investment
lack of interest, concern, or feeling where caring would be appropriate
an expression of sympathy and sorrow offered specifically in response to bereavement or loss
satisfaction in one's own or others' achievements; can be healthy or, in excess, distorting
excessive pride in one's appearance, status, or achievements, focused on how one appears to others
dangerous overconfidence, especially a belief in one's own superiority that invites catastrophic failure
an exaggerated opinion of one's own abilities or intelligence
an exaggerated sense of superiority expressed outward in behavior that demeans others
a realistic and restrained estimate of one's own qualities, without false inflation or false deflation
the willingness to set aside one's ego in service of truth, learning, or other people
the practice of understating or making light of one's own qualities or achievements
lack of confidence in one's own abilities, causing hesitation or reluctance to act
a claim to qualities, status, or importance that one does not actually possess
the feeling that you have done something wrong; tied to an act, not to your identity
the painful feeling that you are fundamentally bad or defective as a person
deep regret and sorrow specifically for one's own wrongdoing, felt rather than merely acknowledged
sorrow for something done, not done, or lost — broader than remorse, not requiring wrongdoing
sincere sorrow for wrongdoing combined with a genuine intention to repair or change
an expression of disappointment or disapproval directed at someone for falling short of a standard
intense embarrassment or shame, usually with a strong social component
feeling or showing genuine sorrow for wrongdoing, with willingness to make amends
righteous anger at perceived injustice or unfair treatment — moral, not merely personal
intense indignation at something shockingly unjust or offensive, demanding a response
the feeling that someone or something is beneath consideration, entirely unworthy of respect
scorn for someone seen as inferior, less extreme than contempt but still rejecting
behavior or speech that implies the other person is less intelligent or capable than oneself
open contempt expressed through mockery or derision — contempt made loud
intense irritation caused by someone or something that keeps frustrating you over time
overwhelming, uncontrolled anger that disables judgment and demands immediate expression
intense, purposeful anger that has decided what it intends to do — slow-building and deliberate
cards.syn: rage with purpose, vengeance, moral fury

cards.definition: a feeling of personal offense at something said or done that affects one's dignity
resentment that has been held for so long it has become part of how someone sees the world
sustained hostility between parties — unlike contempt, it implies the feeling runs both ways
the acute emotional response to loss — specific in its cause, intense, and tied to what is gone
deep, general sadness — broader than grief, not requiring a specific loss
a quiet, reflective sadness that lingers and often has a quality of beauty or contemplation
a bittersweet longing for the past, holding both the pleasure of the memory and the pain of its loss
the process of expressing and working through grief, often with ritual or over time
a state of complete emptiness and isolation caused by loss — grief at its most total
a state of hopelessness and low spirits, especially a sense that things will not improve
a passionate outward expression of grief, regret, or loss — grief given voice
the willingness to act rightly or move forward despite feeling fear — fear felt and acted through
the outward performance of facing danger without visible fear, whether or not fear is felt inwardly
bold willingness to take risks that others would not, carrying both admiration and alarm
acting without regard for consequences — the negative framing of the same behavior that audacity frames positively
acting too quickly without pausing to consider the consequences — recklessness with an emphasis on speed
careful, sensible judgment about what to do — the wisdom to know when not to act
acting suddenly on feeling without pausing for reflection — the personality pattern beneath rashness
deliberately planned for a specific purpose — the opposite of impulsive
showing courage and determination in the face of real difficulty — heroic in effort and spirit
lacking confidence or courage as a settled character tendency — hesitancy as a pattern, not a moment
fearless and adventurous, approaching challenges with eagerness rather than reluctance
wary and careful to consider all circumstances before acting — prudence at its most thorough
truthful and direct, sometimes bluntly so — honesty as a style of communication
direct and unreserved in speech — saying what needs to be said without softening it unnecessarily
genuinely meaning what one says or does — authenticity of feeling rather than accuracy of fact
consistently telling the truth about verifiable facts — the most factual member of this cluster
pretending to be open and honest while actually concealing an agenda or opinion
maintaining two contradictory faces over time — deception as a sustained character pattern
creating a false impression through lies, omissions, or technically-true misdirection
to cause someone to form a false or incorrect understanding, whether through lies or omissions
to control someone through indirect means that bypass their rational judgment
given to habitual lying — dishonesty as a settled character trait rather than a single act
excessively flattering toward those in power in order to gain advantage
to use ambiguous language in order to avoid committing to a position, while remaining technically honest
to speak in an evasive way that shades into active distortion or dishonesty
understood or implied by all parties without being directly stated or written
quick to understand and find solutions — intelligence applied fast, not necessarily deep
having good judgment built from experience and reflection, in service of what actually matters
sharp practical judgment, especially in self-interest — intelligence applied to outcomes
accurately reading situations and people — perceptiveness applied to the social and political world
able to notice and understand things quickly and accurately — especially things others overlook
showing sound judgment and wisdom in practical decisions — thoughtful, fair, and consequence-aware
clever, original, and inventive — finding solutions that didn't exist before
lacking the experience or understanding needed to see complexity — innocence not yet tested
slow to understand things that should be clear — the opposite of perceptive
easily deceived or manipulated because of excessive trust
having rare, penetrating wisdom and good judgment earned through long reflection and experience
lacking foresight, unable to see beyond immediate circumstances — literally and figuratively short-sighted
persistently holding on to something despite difficulty — persistence framed as admirable strength
refusing to change despite pressure or new information — persistence framed as neutral to negative
stubbornly refusing to change in a way that seems unreasonable — more negative than stubborn
refusing to compromise one's position, especially in negotiation or political conflict
showing steady, determined effort in the face of difficulty — persistence through grinding work
admirably firm and unwavering in purpose — persistence as a decision that has been made and kept
unceasingly intense, without pause or mercy — persistence that never stops pushing
firmly committed to a person, principle, or course of action — loyalty and constancy over time
to cease resisting and accept defeat, often formally — the word for the moment persistence ends
continued, sustained effort toward a goal despite difficulty — persistence treated as a virtue
famous and respected because of genuine achievement — the most neutral and elevated positive fame
famous for something bad — widespread recognition for the wrong reasons
well known specifically for a wicked act or quality — often attached to a particular event rather than general reputation
respected and distinguished within a specific field, recognized by peers for genuine expertise
respected and admired for excellence, especially visible or career-long achievement
important or well-known in a public sphere — visible without necessarily implying earned respect
widely praised and admired with enthusiasm — fame that is warm and publicly endorsed
not known or little-known, existing outside the reach of general recognition
highly distinguished through a record of achievement that has made someone genuinely shine
marked with shame and social disapproval in a way that sticks and shapes how others treat you
to cause someone to believe or do something through reasons and argument — the honest form of influence
to persuade someone through sustained flattery, charm, or coaxing rather than argument
to gently encourage someone toward an action through patient reassurance rather than pressure
to charm or enchant someone in a way that involves deception — influence through pleasurable distraction
to make someone afraid in order to influence their behavior — influence through fear or implied threat
to force someone to act through circumstances or authority that remove genuine choice
to attract someone through the offer of something desirable — influence through want
to use an asset or advantage as a mechanism that multiplies your influence or effect
cards.syn: use strategically, capitalize on, apply as force

cards.definition: to cause someone to change their opinion or course — the gentlest word for influence
to shock or excite someone into action — influence that moves people from inertia to motion
showing great attention to every detail, with nothing overlooked or left imprecise
very attentive to accuracy and detail, sometimes to the point of excessive care
diligent and careful to do what is morally right — thoroughness applied to ethics rather than craft
excessively concerned with minor details or rules in a way that misses the point
cards.ant: flexible, practical, seeing the bigger picture

cards.definition: showing steady, careful effort over time — reliable hard work rather than brilliant bursts
complete in every respect, covering all aspects without leaving important things out
hasty and therefore not thorough — insufficient attention caused by not taking enough time
done as a routine without care or investment — technically performed but meaningless in execution
lacking any organizing principle or plan — not just incomplete but without structure behind it
demanding a great deal of care and precision — a standard that only thoroughness and accuracy can meet
of very little importance — not just unimportant but beneath consideration
so small as to be not worth considering — importance measured against effect rather than against priority
at the edge of significance — real, but barely enough to matter
of considerable size or importance — enough to make a real difference
notably large or important, drawing attention by its degree
of great and lasting significance — importance large enough to define what comes after it
more important than everything else — priority that overrides all competing considerations
crucially important in relation to a turning point — not just important but determinative
having great depth of meaning or effect — significance that goes inward rather than upward
occurring as a minor accompaniment to something else — present, but not changing the outcome
passing quickly — brevity without a judgment about value or beauty
lasting for a very short time — brevity with a quality of fragility or beauty
passing swiftly and not lasting — the most everyday word for brief
never ending or never pausing — ongoing without cessation
occurring at intervals, stopping and starting with some underlying pattern
occurring occasionally and without predictable pattern — more unpredictable than intermittent
happening repeatedly with breaks in between — recurrence, not constant presence
without any interruption or break — unbroken duration
lasting over a long period, having survived the tests of time and circumstance
not permanent and understood to be so — brevity that is expected and accepted by all parties
confidently direct in expressing one's position or needs — the positive middle of the confidence spectrum
direct and proactively honest — saying what needs to be said without waiting to be asked
bold in a shameless or shocking way, with disregard for social norms or expected restraint
showing a lack of proper respect, especially toward those in authority — boldness that has crossed into disrespect
overstepping the limits of what one is entitled to, confusing one's own status or permission
unpleasantly domineering — confidence that has become oppressive and eliminates space for others
showing respectful submission to someone else's judgment, authority, or wishes
excessively submissive in a way that suggests loss of dignity — deference without self-respect
excessively attentive to those in power, using deference as a deliberate strategy for advantage
not drawing attention to oneself or one's achievements — simply not putting yourself forward
not revealing thoughts or feelings readily — choosing not to speak rather than being unable to
slow to reveal emotions or opinions as a matter of temperament, not deliberate choice
saying what you think without softening it — directness that prioritizes honesty over social ease
skilled at saying difficult things in a way that minimizes unnecessary offense
the result or outcome of a cause — the noun that pairs with the verb 'affect'
to consist of or be made up of — the whole comprises the parts
cards.syn: consist of, include, be made up of

cards.definition: to make up or constitute something — the parts compose the whole
impartial and unbiased, having no personal stake in the outcome — a virtue, not a lack of interest
not interested or engaged — the absence of curiosity or attention
an expression of praise or admiration for someone or their work
the most important person in an organization or agreement; or most important as an adjective
reluctant and unwilling to do something — describing one's disposition toward an action
to feel intense hatred or disgust for something or someone
famous or important in history — actually part of the historical record, not merely old
relating to or concerning the past — anything from history, significant or not
relating to physical pleasure experienced through the senses — tactile, gustatory, olfactory
appealing to the aesthetic senses in a beautiful, refined way — not physical pleasure but aesthetic appreciation
fear of losing to a rival something you already possess or feel entitled to — the guarding emotion
the desire to have what someone else has — the wanting emotion that looks outward and upward
to desire something belonging to another with a moral weight that implies you shouldn't
cards.syn: desire what belongs to another, envy, crave

cards.definition: to feel a strong, aching desire — usually for something absent, distant, or beyond reach
cards.syn: long for, pine for, ache for

cards.definition: feeling a gentle, melancholy longing for something past or out of reach
pitifully sad and isolated, or holding a faint hope that is unlikely to be realized
having intense and passionate feeling — heated, committed, and burning with its cause
enthusiastic and passionate, especially in feeling or belief — warm and sustained
showing strong and forceful feeling expressed with intensity and insistence
showing great energy and enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause — passion applied to purpose
sincere and serious in intent — not passionate in temperature but genuine in commitment
to feel envious of someone's possession of something; or to give unwillingly
actively curious and questioning — the behavioral expression of curiosity rather than the feeling of it
cautious and watchful because of a specific awareness of possible danger or threat
generous and forgiving, especially toward those with less power or those who have wronged you
generous in material giving or in how you interpret others' actions and intentions
sparing with money or resources as a deliberate practice — careful management, not deprivation
severely simple in manner or lifestyle, stripped of comfort or decoration
reluctant to spend even when spending is appropriate — frugality turned mean and self-defeating
unconventional in behavior or appearance in ways others may find charming, interesting, or strange
peculiar to one particular individual or thing — personal strangeness, not social performance
enduring difficulty without complaint or visible emotion — not unfeeling, but not ruled by feeling
dealing with things practically, focused on what works rather than on ideals or principles
permissive and tolerant in judgment or enforcement — applying rules at the lighter end
strict and demanding, allowing little or no deviation from the required standard
designed to attract attention and impress others in a showy way — display for its own sake
not in harmony with its surroundings — the thing that doesn't fit, creating visible dissonance
clear, logical, and convincing — an argument that holds together from first premise to last conclusion
clearly expressed and easy to understand — writing or thought that lets light through
intelligently analytical, cutting directly to what matters without circling the problem
vigorous and to the point, especially in criticism — sharp and forceful together
having great knowledge from wide reading and study — learning that shows in how ideas connect
having a penetrating insight into things — the ability to see what others miss
superficially plausible but actually wrong — a convincing fake that falls apart under examination
concisely and meaningfully expressed — saying what counts in as few words as possible
using very few words — the most extreme form of conciseness, sometimes devastating
a short, compressed statement that expresses a general truth — thinking reduced to its bone
sharp and cutting in expression, especially in criticism — wit with an edge
sharply satirical or darkly humorous — acerbity with a black edge
brief to the point of apparent rudeness — brevity that has an edge to it
not direct or explicit — approaching a subject from the side rather than head-on
having a hidden or mysterious meaning, deliberately withholding as much as it reveals
not clearly understood — impenetrable to understanding, whether through complexity or intent
the process of arriving at truth through the tension and resolution of opposing ideas
cards.syn: dialogue, reasoned debate, synthesis through opposition

cards.definition: clever but misleading reasoning — using the tools of logic to reach conclusions logic doesn't support
cards.syn: specious reasoning, fallacious argument, intellectual deception

cards.definition: elegant and precise in expression, as if cut in stone — refined to its essential form
to think deeply and at length, especially about something that resists quick resolution
translucently clear in expression or thought — clarity so complete it reveals the quality of the mind behind it
excessive concern with minor academic rules and details to the point of losing the purpose behind them
to think carefully and at length — deliberate, sustained thought as distinct from passing consideration
to formally inform someone so they are fully up to date on what they need to know
calmness and composure, especially in difficult situations — not the absence of feeling but the refusal to be ruled by it
patient self-control — the restraint of an impulse to retaliate or react, held as a deliberate practice
morally correct behavior — the quality of being straight in one's moral life, consistently and visibly
the quality of strong moral principles, especially honesty and incorruptibility — the virtue sought in those trusted with power
impossible to subdue or defeat — a quality of spirit that simply does not give in, regardless of circumstance
showing genuine unselfishness in concern for others' well-being — giving without calculating the return
well-meaning and kindly toward others — goodwill as a sustained disposition, not just a gesture
accorded great respect through the combination of age, wisdom, and sustained excellence
deceitful and untrustworthy — guilty of betrayal by someone who had a duty of loyalty
having a habit so deeply established by long practice that it has become part of identity
overindulging in pleasure without regard for morality — the character that has given itself to its appetites
willingness to act dishonorably in exchange for money — corruption in its most commercial form
deserving strong moral condemnation — not merely wrong but worthy of public censure
deserving or causing public disgrace — failure or wrongdoing that destroys reputation
not susceptible to corruption or bribery — integrity that has been tested and has held
the practice of maintaining two contradictory faces — deception as a sustained way of operating
not able to be doubted, questioned, or criticized — credibility or integrity so complete that challenges do not stick
loyal, reliable, and hard-working over a long period — devotion that doesn't require recognition to continue
formidable in a way that earns respect — especially impressive as an opponent or competitor
formal or public expression of strong disapproval — stronger than criticism, weaker than punishment
eager or quick to argue or fight — aggressive before being provoked rather than in response to provocation
making false or damaging claims in a deliberately aggressive way — attacks designed to harm without justification
generosity and forgiveness toward those with less power or those who have wronged you — the most admirable form of power
impossible to appease or satisfy — opposition or resolve that simply does not soften under pressure
so beautiful, vast, or impressive as to inspire awe — beauty that overwhelms rather than merely pleases
too great, extreme, or beautiful to be expressed in words — the experience that reaches the limit of language
going beyond ordinary experience or understanding — surpassing what categories can normally contain
radiating light or radiating clarity — brilliance that illuminates what surrounds it
bringing strong images, memories, or feelings to mind — conjuring rather than describing
relating to deep internal feeling — a response that bypasses the intellect and goes directly to the body
expressing emotion in a beautiful, musical way — writing or speech that moves as well as informs
beautiful or poignant in a way that stays with you long after the encounter is over
severely simple and unadorned — beauty achieved by stripping everything away until only what matters remains
simple without decoration or excess, lean by design — controlled in a way that makes the absence visible
striking in a way that stops you — demanding attention before you've decided to give it
extremely delicate and light — beauty that seems barely to belong to the material world
sweet and musical in sound — a quality of voice or music that flows like honey
having a mournful, commemorative quality — the tone that honors what is gone with both sorrow and love
evoking a keen sense of sadness or regret, often with beauty woven through it — sharp and sweet at once
glowing intensely — with heat, passion, or anger — a brightness generated from within
characterized by overwhelming joy or enthusiasm — happiness beyond containment
expressing sorrow in a mournful, musical way — grief given shape through sound
showing luminous colors that shift and change when seen from different angles
shining brilliantly — splendor that insists on itself and fills the space it occupies
impossible to stop or prevent — a force that cannot be delayed, redirected, or argued with
of enormous scale or effect — change that reorganizes the landscape it passes through
strongly influencing later developments — the work or event that plants the seeds of everything that follows
a turning point after which everything is different — the moment that divides before and after
cards.syn: turning point, pivotal moment, dividing line

cards.definition: having knowledge of events before they happen — foresight that turns out to have been accurate
forming or marking a significant new epoch — change that defines the era it belongs to
of great significance, especially with a sense of ominous implication — something that feels like a warning
a person or thing that signals something to come — the early sign of a coming change
proceeding gradually in a harmful way that is not immediately apparent — danger that looks harmless at first
having a gradually harmful effect — damage that is real but slow, eroding rather than striking
unchanging and unable to be changed — fixed in a way that resists all the forces that change other things
to cause something to happen suddenly and before it is ready — triggering events faster than anyone planned
involving a sudden, large-scale upheaval — change that destroys the conditions it moves through
just coming into existence — at the earliest stage of development, full of possibility and fragility
remaining as a trace of something that has otherwise disappeared — the remnant of an earlier form
the defining spirit or mood of a particular period — the invisible force that shapes what a generation thinks and feels
cards.syn: spirit of the age, cultural mood, prevailing atmosphere

cards.definition: not able to be changed, reversed, or recovered — the quality of decisions that cannot be untaken
existing but not yet manifest or active — present before it is visible
beginning to develop — at the very earliest stage of becoming, barely begun
having important effects that extend beyond the immediate — weight that is felt downstream
unable to be resisted or avoided — an outcome the logic of the situation cannot escape
involving sudden, violent upheaval — change that arrives with the violence and disorder of a spasm
likely to arouse resentment through perceived unfairness — distinction or treatment that demeans
tending to obstruct or harm — opposition that is structural rather than personal
a plot or scheme, usually devious and sustained — deception organized and architectural
strongly committed to a party or cause to the point where objectivity is compromised
subject to sudden, unpredictable changes of mood — a temperament that keeps everyone around it off-balance
given to sudden and arbitrary changes — unpredictability that seems to lack even internal logic
liable to change rapidly and for the worse — instability with an edge of danger
stubbornly resistant to authority or attempts to change — obstinacy directed at control itself
demonstrating aggression and willingness to fight — the posture of those seeking rather than avoiding conflict
intended to make peace or reduce hostility — a gesture or tone designed to move toward resolution
irritable, quarrelsome, and difficult to manage — a group or dynamic where conflict is the default
angry and bitter in tone — the register of conflict that has moved from disagreement into personal rancor
arrogant and domineering — confidence in one's authority so complete that obedience is assumed, not requested
dominance of one group over others, especially through control of what is considered normal
a leader who gains power by appealing to emotions and prejudices rather than reason or evidence
a strong argument against a particular opinion — advocacy that has abandoned balance in favor of demolition
a forceful and bitter verbal attack — polemic made angry, sustained beyond argument into denunciation
filled with bitter criticism or malice — language designed to corrode and damage
designed to inflame conflict or strong emotion — communication that sets rather than illuminates
too difficult to manage or resolve — problems and people that resist all efforts to change them
stubbornly refusing to change — resistance as a fundamental stance, not a negotiating position
promoting a particular cause or point of view through what is selected and how it is framed
a sudden, complete realization — understanding that arrives all at once rather than being reasoned toward
the release of strong emotion through art or experience — purging through feeling rather than suppression
listlessness and dissatisfaction arising from a lack of occupation or meaning — existential boredom
a pleasant tiredness or dreamy relaxation — exhaustion made comfortable
a state of being pleasantly lost in thought — the dreaming half of waking consciousness
comfort or consolation in a time of distress — the specific form of relief that actually helps
the occurrence of beneficial events by chance — finding good things you were not looking for
cards.syn: happy accident, fortunate chance, lucky discovery

cards.definition: optimistic, especially in a difficult situation — cheerful confidence that doesn't depend on the evidence
calm and not easily excited — a temperament that simply doesn't generate the extremes that others do
self-confidence and composure, especially in difficult situations — poise that doesn't require things to be going well
the examination of one's own thoughts, feelings, and motivations — looking inward rather than outward
the disappointment that follows the discovery that something is not as good as believed — the price of illusion
indecision that shows up as movement between options — ambivalence expressed through behavior
a feeling of guilt or moral scruple about something — the twinge of conscience before or after a questionable act
unable to remain still, especially under restriction — the energy of someone or something held back
engaged in long, unhappy thoughts — reflection that has turned inward on a wound
formal release from guilt or moral consequence — what contrition seeks and forgiveness gives
physical or mental weariness — exhaustion that has depleted the will as well as the body
a firmly held belief that has been tested and held; or a finding of guilty in court
sustained, often circular thought about something troubling — the cognitive state of someone absorbed in a problem that won't resolve
the quality of being optimistic and cheerful — sanguine as a character property rather than a mood
relating to a transitional state between two conditions — the threshold space where one thing has ended and another hasn't yet begun
experienced through another person rather than directly — real in feeling, borrowed in origin
weak or slight — a connection, argument, or position that barely holds
complicated and difficult to navigate — complexity organized in a way that makes orientation almost impossible
not genuine, designed to appear so — false in a way that requires examination to reveal
a person who cultivates a superficial interest in a subject — engagement without the commitment that produces depth
commonplace and lacking imagination — ordinary in a way that makes no claim to anything more
so lacking in originality that it is obvious and boring — familiar to the point of exhaustion
an overused statement that sounds wise but has lost its meaning through repetition
overused to the point of losing its original effect — exhausted by repetition
done without good reason and uncalled for — the element that serves no purpose the work requires
more than what is needed — excess that clutters without contributing
understood by or intended for only a small group with specialist knowledge — accessible in principle but requiring preparation
understood by very few, with a quality of mystery or antiquity — knowledge that feels older and stranger than mere specialism
like an idyll — perfectly happy and peaceful in a way that belongs to pastoral fantasy
belonging to a period other than the one being portrayed or occurring in — out of its proper time
exceedingly idealistic and impractical — noble in ambition and unlikely in outcome
regularly found or prevalent in a particular group or place — belonging to the system rather than arriving from outside
a solution claimed to cure all problems — the universal remedy that doesn't exist
apparently attractive but having no real value — flashy in a way that doesn't survive inspection
going beyond what is known into reasoned imagination — thinking at the frontier where evidence runs out
many and varied — a multiplicity of different kinds existing together
the ability to make good judgments quickly in practical situations — wisdom that works at speed
dignity, seriousness, and weight of manner — a presence that commands attention without demanding it
habitual truthfulness — the quality of being truthful as a settled character property rather than a situational choice
countless or extremely large in number — so many that counting is beside the point
able to be felt or touched; or so obvious and present as to seem almost physical
stated or appearing to be true, but not necessarily reflecting what is actually the case
behavior or speech that is artificial and designed to impress — a performance of a quality one doesn't possess
the outward appearance of something, especially when the reality differs from the presentation
the face of something concealing what lies behind it — a maintained presentation that differs from the interior
to pretend to have a feeling, quality, or condition — the deliberate performance of a state one doesn't have
to conceal or disguise one's real feelings or intentions — removing the true from view rather than substituting the false
the quality of seeming true or real — the texture of believability that makes a story feel possible
a revival of interest, activity, or learning — the return to life of something that had diminished
giving one's name to something — or, of something, being named after the person it is associated with
deriving ideas, methods, or tastes from a wide range of sources rather than following any single approach
the quality of having a ready and penetrating insight — the capacity that perspicacious describes
second to last — the one immediately before the final one
an unpredictable change in circumstances or fortune — the turns that cannot be anticipated and must simply be endured
cards.syn: change of fortune, reversal, unpredictable shift

cards.definition: most notable or important — the feature that stands out above the surrounding detail
clearly expressed and easy to understand — transparency of meaning that makes the structure of thought visible
present or appearing everywhere — so pervasive as to define the landscape it inhabits
open to more than one interpretation, often deliberately — ambiguity used strategically
difficult to interpret or understand — mystery that invites inquiry rather than frustrating it
difficult to understand because genuinely intricate — complexity earned by the subject, not imposed by the writer
difficult to find, catch, or define — resistant to being pinned down even when clearly present
a typical example or model; or the set of assumptions underlying an entire field of inquiry
the original model from which later versions derive — the pattern that recurs because it is fundamental
behavior that conforms to accepted social standards — the unwritten rules governing conduct in formal or shared spaces
showing great attention to correct behavior or detail, especially in matters of etiquette and form
in accordance with the highest standards — faultless in a way that cannot be criticized
tending to talk a great deal — voluble in a way that can be engaging or exhausting
reserved and saying little — brevity as a settled temperament rather than a deliberate choice
the use of many words where fewer would do — verbosity in the specific service of avoiding commitment
giving or indicating a good chance of success — circumstances that favor what you are trying to do
suggesting a good start and a favorable future — a beginning that feels fortunate
a strong feeling that something bad is about to happen — not paranoia but a felt sense of approaching difficulty
to be a sign or warning of something to come — to signal in advance
liable to change — responsive to circumstances in a way that is adaptive rather than unstable
lasting or recurring across years and generations — reliably present in each new period
lasting longer than expected or desired — a process extended beyond the point where it should have resolved
continuing without pause or interruption — the form of continuity that wears things down
following a longer or more indirect route than necessary — going around when going through would be shorter
speaking fluently and at length — the positive register of loquacious, emphasizing flow rather than excess
wisdom and good judgment developed through long experience and deep reflection — the quality sagacious describes
not conducive to success — a beginning or context that doesn't favor what you're trying to do
